layout_insights.hero_style: Full-screen hero with a background scene photo/illustration covered by a strong color overlay gradient, large bold all-caps headline centered (2 lines), a small trust badge/label above the headline, a subtitle paragraph below, and two CTA buttons side by side (filled primary + outlined phone call). Below the buttons sits an inline quick-quote form with 2-3 input fields and a submit button — all within the hero.
layout_insights.nav_style: Transparent-sticky bar with a small logo on the far left, navigation links spread across the center-right, and a phone number CTA button (outlined or filled) on the far right. Nav links include a Services dropdown. The bar sits over the hero background at the top.
layout_insights.services_layout: Two primary patterns used in tandem: (1) a 4-column icon trust/feature grid — each column has a centered thin-line icon, a bold short heading, and a 2–3 line description paragraph; (2) alternating split rows with a large image or illustration on one side (60% width) and a heading with checklist bullet points plus a CTA button on the other side. A colored vertical bar/pipe accents the section heading on split rows.
layout_insights.section_flow: Hero (full-screen with form) → trust/feature 4-column icon grid → split content section (image left, text + checklist right) → large stat/number counter section → another 'why it matters' section with bold all-caps heading → CTA strip → footer

element_insights.card_style: Light-background icon cards with no visible border and no shadow — clean flat columns with centered alignment. Each card has an icon, bold heading, and short paragraph. On split sections, cards are replaced by checklist rows using colored circular checkmarks (filled) alongside short feature descriptions.
element_insights.button_style: Two button styles coexist: (1) solid filled rounded-rectangle primary CTA ('Get a Free Quote'); (2) outlined rounded-rectangle with an icon prefix for phone calls ('Call Now: XXX'). Buttons appear side by side in the hero. A third submit button inside inline forms is a solid filled pill with a high-contrast label.
element_insights.section_separators: Sections alternate between a white/light background and a slightly tinted or image-overlaid background — no hard geometric dividers. The hero bleeds into the page and the transitions are implicit through background shifts.
element_insights.decorative_elements: Hero has a full-bleed background image with a strong gradient color overlay (semi-transparent) creating depth. Section headings in split rows have a short thick vertical colored bar on the left edge. Large bold numbers (e.g., '2,900') are used as visual anchors in stat sections. A small all-caps label with tight tracking ('WHY IT MATTERS', 'WHY CHOOSE US') precedes section headings throughout.
element_insights.icon_style: Thin-line centered icons, approximately 32–40px, single accent color, placed above the heading in 4-column trust grids. Simple and minimal — one icon per feature.

typography_insights.headline_treatment: Hero headline is very large, bold, all-caps, 2 lines, tight line-height (~0.95–1.0). Section headings outside the hero are large bold mixed-case or all-caps, still dominant but slightly smaller. Strong weight contrast between headlines and body.
typography_insights.body_treatment: Body text is small (15–16px), normal weight, neutral muted color, generous line-height (~1.7). Used for descriptions under icons, checklist items, and paragraph blocks in split sections.
typography_insights.special_treatments: Section pre-labels appear as small all-caps text with wide letter-spacing and sometimes a leading bullet or icon dot. Split-section headings have a thick vertical colored bar on the left edge. Stats sections feature oversized bold numbers as the visual centerpiece with a small descriptor label below.

animation_insights.specific_patterns: Service/feature icon columns fade and slide up on scroll entry with a short stagger delay between columns. Hero headline and subtitle appear with a quick fade-in on load. Stat/number counters animate from zero upward when scrolled into view. CTA buttons have a subtle background-darken or scale on hover. Split-section images may have a mild parallax or scale-in on scroll entry. Checklist items reveal sequentially with a slight stagger.

design_direction: The client clearly favors a professional home-services style website with a full-screen hero featuring a background scene covered by a strong color overlay, a large bold all-caps centered headline, a trust badge above the headline, dual CTA buttons (solid fill + outlined phone), and an inline quick-quote form embedded directly in the hero — avoid minimal typographic or abstract heroes. Below the hero, the layout uses a clean 4-column icon trust grid (thin-line icons, bold headings, short descriptions) before transitioning into alternating split sections where a large image or illustration sits left and a heading with checklist bullet points and a CTA button sits right. Section headings consistently use a small all-caps pre-label with wide tracking above them, and split-section headings have a thick vertical colored bar on the left edge. Stat/number sections with oversized bold counters should be included as a social-proof anchor. The nav must be transparent-sticky with a logo on the left, links in the center-right, and a phone CTA button on the far right. Animation should be moderate and smooth: scroll-reveal fade-up with stagger on grid columns, counter animations on stat numbers, and subtle hover lifts on buttons. Do NOT use heavy card shadows, accordion layouts, carousels, or bento grids — the client prefers clean split rows and uniform icon columns. The overall feeling is balanced and trustworthy: professional without being cold, energetic without being flashy.
